lead: Compact CMS-ready version based on EN 301 549-oriented accessibility findings produced by Web Auditor.
copySuccess: The HTML content has been copied to the clipboard.
copyError: Copy failed. Please check browser permissions.
generatedNotice: This page is generated automatically and should be reviewed before publication.
sectionWhy: 1. Why are you receiving this report?
whyParagraphs: This page provides a simplified summary of the accessibility findings identified during the audit of the website.
The content is framed against the EN 301 549 European standard and is intended to make it easier to update a public audit report page.
sectionHow: 2. How should this report be read and understood?
howParagraphs: The status shown below is an indicative status calculated from the accessibility findings produced by Web Auditor.
The tables group technical findings by rule and show the number of impacted pages as well as one example URL.
howBullets: This simplified report focuses only on accessibility findings and intentionally excludes SEO, security and performance findings.
The technical messages in the appendix are kept exactly as emitted by the audit engine.
Human validation is still required before any official publication.
summaryLead: The summary below is based only on accessibility findings detected during the audit.

statusDescriptions.compliant: No accessibility findings were detected by the checks included in this summary.
statusDescriptions.partiallyCompliant: Accessibility warnings were detected. Human review is still required before drawing a final conclusion.
statusDescriptions.nonCompliant: At least one accessibility finding with error severity was detected by the checks included in this summary.

noFindings: No accessibility findings were detected for this summary.
findingsLead: Findings are grouped by technical code to make editorial publication easier.
auditDetailsIntro: The tables below group accessibility findings by audit source. The editorial structure and layout are aligned with the Smals reference page while using EN 301 549 positioning.
appendixParagraph: This appendix keeps the raw engine messages to help prepare more editorial wording in the CMS.
rawMessagesNotice: The messages below are displayed verbatim and may require manual rewriting.
criteriaTitle: EN 301 549 criteria automatically checked by Axe
criteriaLead: The table below exhaustively lists the EN 301 549 criteria covered by Axe Core. Each row shows whether a non-compliance was detected during the audit and includes reference links to Axe documentation.

1.title: Perceivable
1.description: Information and user interface components must be presented to users in ways they can perceive.

2.title: Operable
2.description: User interface components and navigation must be operable.

3.title: Understandable
3.description: Information and the operation of the user interface must be understandable.

4.title: Robust
4.description: Content must be robust enough that it can be interpreted reliably by a wide variety of user agents, including assistive technologies.

1.1.title: Text Alternatives
1.1.description: Provide text alternatives for non-text content.

1.3.title: Adaptable
1.3.description: Create content that can be presented in different ways without losing information or structure.

1.4.title: Distinguishable
1.4.description: Make it easier for users to see and hear content.

2.1.title: Keyboard Accessible
2.1.description: Make all functionality available from a keyboard.

2.2.title: Enough Time
2.2.description: Provide users enough time to read and use content.

2.4.title: Navigable
2.4.description: Provide ways to help users navigate, find content, and determine where they are.

2.5.title: Input Modalities
2.5.description: Make it easier for users to operate functionality through inputs beyond keyboard.

3.1.title: Readable
3.1.description: Make text content readable and understandable.

3.3.title: Input Assistance
3.3.description: Help users avoid and correct mistakes.

4.1.title: Compatible
4.1.description: Maximize compatibility with current and future user agents, including assistive technologies.
